title: Hilton says 'CAT' ferry study is 'positive' as province looks to future | Surge 105
description:
Yarmouth’s MLA says there’s a positive outlook for the ‘CAT’ ferry.
The province released an economic impact study on the Nova Scotia-to-Maine service last week.
It said passengers travelling to the province on the ‘CAT’ stay longer, and spend more money, and there’s support for the service across Nova Scotia.
Nick Hilton says they’re excited for 2026, and the province will be looking at options for the future.
He says the government has been very supportive of the service, and the impact study was commissioned to make sure tax dollars are being invested correctly.
text:
Yarmouth’s MLA says there’s a positive outlook for the ‘CAT’ ferry.

The province released an economic impact study on the Nova Scotia-to-Maine service last week.

It said passengers travelling to the province on the ‘CAT’ stay longer, and spend more money, and there’s support for the service across Nova Scotia.

Nick Hilton says they’re excited for 2026, and the province will be looking at options for the future.

“And I’ll be working alongside the department to see what those options are, but overall we’re still feeling very positive, and the report helps reflect that,” said Hilton.

He says the government has been very supportive of the service, and the impact study was commissioned to make sure tax dollars are being invested correctly.

Yarmouth Mayor Pam Mood has called on the province to sign a long term deal, noting that tourism operators want clarity on the future.

title: Business Jet Market Size and Forecast 2026–2034 | Trader - Vocal Media
description:
The global Business Jet Market is poised for steady expansion over the next decade.
Business Jet Industry Overview The business jet industry encompasses the design, manufacturing, and operation of aircraft primarily used for executive, corporate, and private travel.
Regional Market Insights United States Business Jet Market The United States remains the largest and most mature business jet market globally.
Germany Business Jet Market Germany represents a core European business aviation hub.
Saudi Arabia Business Jet Market Saudi Arabia’s business jet sector is expanding rapidly under Vision 2030 reforms.
text:
The global Business Jet Market is poised for steady expansion over the next decade. According to Renub Research, the market is projected to grow from US$ 20.82 billion in 2025 to US$ 29.15 billion by 2034, registering a CAGR of 3.81% from 2026 to 2034.

Request Sample Report:

This growth trajectory reflects a structural shift in global mobility preferences. Increasing corporate globalization, a rising population of high-net-worth individuals (HNWIs), and demand for time-efficient travel are redefining air transportation. Alongside outright ownership, models such as fractional ownership, jet cards, and charter services are broadening access to private aviation, expanding the addressable market.

Business Jet Industry Overview

The business jet industry encompasses the design, manufacturing, and operation of aircraft primarily used for executive, corporate, and private travel. Unlike commercial aviation, business jets provide on-demand, point-to-point connectivity, enabling passengers to bypass rigid airline schedules and congested airports.

These aircraft are generally classified into:

Very Light Jets (VLJs)

Light Jets

Mid-Size Jets

Super Mid-Size Jets

Large-Cabin Jets

Each category serves a distinct segment—from first-time private users and SMEs to multinational corporations and government entities requiring intercontinental range and cabin capacity.

Business jets also serve specialized roles such as:

Air ambulance and medical evacuation

Government and diplomatic missions

Military transport

Limited cargo operations

Technological advancements are significantly reshaping the sector. Modern aircraft feature advanced avionics, improved fuel efficiency, lightweight composite materials, and cabin innovations offering high-speed connectivity and workspace optimization. Sustainability is increasingly central to manufacturer strategy, with ongoing investments in sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), hybrid propulsion research, and noise-reduction technologies.

Despite high capital intensity and regulatory oversight, the business jet industry remains a resilient and strategically important segment of global aviation.

Growth Drivers in the Business Jet Market

1. Rising Demand for Time-Efficient Travel

In today’s fast-moving global economy, time is a premium asset. Corporate executives and ultra-high-net-worth individuals prioritize flexibility, speed, and privacy. Business jets eliminate layovers, long airport queues, and restricted airline schedules, allowing direct access to secondary airports closer to final destinations.

For multinational companies, reduced travel time enhances productivity and accelerates decision-making cycles. Meanwhile, affluent individuals increasingly prefer private aviation for leisure travel, family mobility, and security concerns.

This structural demand for personalized and efficient mobility continues to support long-term market growth.

2. Technological Advancements and Aircraft Innovation

Technology remains a cornerstone of industry expansion. New-generation business jets integrate:

Advanced avionics systems

Predictive maintenance solutions

AI-assisted navigation tools

Fuel-efficient engines

Enhanced cabin connectivity

Aircraft manufacturers are improving aerodynamics and incorporating lightweight materials to increase range while reducing fuel burn. Cabin innovation now focuses equally on productivity and luxury, transforming jets into flying boardrooms and private retreats.

Sustainability initiatives—including hybrid propulsion and SAF compatibility—are attracting environmentally conscious buyers and ensuring regulatory compliance in an era of tightening emissions standards.

3. Growth of Fractional Ownership and Charter Services

One of the most transformative trends in the industry is the democratization of private aviation through fractional ownership and charter models.

Fractional ownership allows multiple customers to share aircraft costs while maintaining usage flexibility. Jet cards and on-demand charter services eliminate long-term commitments, attracting SMEs and emerging-market executives.

This shift broadens market participation beyond traditional ultra-wealthy buyers. It also increases aircraft utilization rates, benefiting operators and manufacturers alike.

Key Market Challenges

High Operational and Maintenance Costs

Business jets require substantial upfront capital investment, often exceeding tens of millions of dollars. Ongoing expenses—including fuel, maintenance, crew salaries, insurance, hangar fees, and regulatory compliance—remain high.

Fuel price volatility further complicates cost management. Even fractional and charter models, while more accessible, still involve significant operational expenditures.

Cost optimization remains critical for sustaining profitability across the value chain.

Regulatory and Environmental Pressures

Compliance with aviation authorities such as the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) and the European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) involves rigorous certification and inspection requirements. These processes increase costs and may delay deliveries.

Additionally, environmental scrutiny has intensified. Business jets face criticism regarding carbon emissions per passenger. Manufacturers must invest heavily in sustainable technologies to meet evolving standards and mitigate reputational risk.

Balancing innovation, compliance, and profitability will be key to sustained growth.

Regional Market Insights

United States Business Jet Market

The United States remains the largest and most mature business jet market globally. Strong corporate presence, high wealth concentration, and well-developed private aviation infrastructure drive sustained demand.

Major hubs such as New York, Los Angeles, and Miami dominate operations. Fleet modernization and high adoption of fractional and charter services continue to support expansion.

Leading U.S.-based manufacturers such as Textron Inc., General Dynamics Corp, and Honda Motor Co Ltd play significant roles in shaping global competitiveness.

Despite high operating costs, strong economic fundamentals and innovation leadership sustain U.S. dominance.

Germany Business Jet Market

Germany represents a core European business aviation hub. Cities like Frankfurt and Munich anchor corporate travel demand, supported by a strong industrial base.

The market benefits from increasing charter demand and a growing ultra-high-net-worth population. However, stringent European environmental regulations and operational costs influence purchasing decisions.

European manufacturers such as Airbus SE and Dassault Aviation SA maintain strong presence in the regional ecosystem.

China Business Jet Market

China remains one of Asia-Pacific’s largest business aviation markets, though growth has fluctuated due to economic shifts and regulatory changes.

Expanding general aviation infrastructure and improved FBO services are strengthening operational efficiency. While outright ownership remains attractive among HNWIs, charter demand is rising.

Brazilian manufacturer Embraer SA continues expanding its footprint in Asia, reflecting the region’s long-term potential.

Saudi Arabia Business Jet Market

Saudi Arabia’s business jet sector is expanding rapidly under Vision 2030 reforms. Increased luxury tourism, corporate mobility, and aviation infrastructure investment are driving growth.

Enhanced regulatory frameworks and improved private aviation terminals are attracting both domestic and international operators. The Kingdom is emerging as one of the most dynamic private aviation markets in the Middle East.

Recent Developments in the Business Jet Market

Industry players are accelerating innovation and fleet expansion:

Honda Aircraft Co., a subsidiary of Honda Motor Co Ltd, began manufacturing its first test unit of the aircraft formerly known as the HondaJet 2600 concept in October 2025.

In May 2025, Bombardier Inc. successfully conducted the inaugural flight of its first production Global 8000 aircraft.

In February 2025, Embraer SA signed a major purchase agreement with Flexjet, including 182 aircraft with options for 30 more.

In October 2024, Textron Inc. introduced the Cessna Citation M2 Gen3, CJ3 Gen3, and CJ4 Gen3 models featuring advanced Garmin avionics and Emergency Autoland technology.

These developments reflect strong manufacturer confidence and forward-looking fleet strategies.

Market Segmentation

By Type:

Light

Medium

Large

By Business Model:

On-demand Service

Ownership

By Application:

Propulsion System

Aero Structure

Cabin Interiors

Avionics

Others

By Region:

North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Latin America, Middle East & Africa

Countries analyzed include the United States, Canada, France, Germany, United Kingdom, China, Japan, India, Australia, Brazil, Mexico, Saudi Arabia, and South Africa, among others.

Competitive Landscape

Key players covered from five viewpoints (Overview, Key Persons, Recent Developments, SWOT Analysis, Financial Insights):

Airbus SE

Bombardier Inc.

Dassault Aviation SA

Embraer SA

General Dynamics Corp

Honda Motor Co Ltd

Pilatus Aircraft Ltd

Textron Inc.

Competition centers around range capability, fuel efficiency, cabin innovation, sustainability alignment, and lifecycle service offerings.

Final Thoughts

The Business Jet Market is entering a phase of structured, sustainable expansion. While not characterized by explosive growth, the projected rise to US$ 29.15 billion by 2034 reflects resilient demand supported by globalization, wealth concentration, and corporate mobility needs.

Technology, sustainability, and flexible ownership models will define the next decade. Manufacturers that balance innovation with regulatory compliance and cost efficiency will maintain competitive advantage

title: Ekosistem SAF Terverifikasi Global, Pertamina Siap Masuk Pasar Internasional
description:
Brussel-Belgia, OG Indonesia -- PT Pertamina (Persero) mempertegas langkahnya dalam membangun ekosistem Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) yang terverifikasi secara global dan siap merambah pasar internasional, termasuk Eropa dan Asia-Pasifik.
Dalam forum tersebut, Pertamina berbagi panggung dengan para pemimpin produsen SAF global seperti Neste, EcoCeres, dan Montana Renewables.
“Forum ini mempertemukan produsen SAF global, pelaku industri penerbangan, dan pemangku kepentingan keberlanjutan dunia.
Kehadiran Pertamina di forum ini menunjukkan bahwa Indonesia tidak hanya menjadi pasar, tetapi juga bagian dari solusi global dalam dekarbonisasi sektor penerbangan.
Pertamina menilai tantangan utama pengembangan SAF global saat ini bukan lagi pada teknologi pengolahan, melainkan ketersediaan bahan baku berkelanjutan.
text:
Brussel-Belgia, OG Indonesia -- PT Pertamina (Persero) mempertegas langkahnya dalam membangun ekosistem Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) yang terverifikasi secara global dan siap merambah pasar internasional, termasuk Eropa dan Asia-Pasifik. Langkah strategis ini merupakan komitmen nyata Pertamina untuk mengakselerasi dekarbonisasi sektor penerbangan, sekaligus memposisikan Indonesia sebagai pemain strategis dalam transisi energi global.

Hal tersebut disampaikan oleh Direktur Transformasi dan Keberlanjutan Bisnis Pertamina Agung Wicaksono, dalam diskusi panel internasional bertajuk “Behind the Blend: The Producers Making Net-Zero Aviation Possible” pada ISCC Global Sustainability Conference 2026 di Brussel, Belgia. Dalam forum tersebut, Pertamina berbagi panggung dengan para pemimpin produsen SAF global seperti Neste, EcoCeres, dan Montana Renewables.

“Forum ini mempertemukan produsen SAF global, pelaku industri penerbangan, dan pemangku kepentingan keberlanjutan dunia. Kehadiran Pertamina di forum ini menunjukkan bahwa Indonesia tidak hanya menjadi pasar, tetapi juga bagian dari solusi global dalam dekarbonisasi sektor penerbangan. Kami siap memasuki pasar global melalui SAF,” ujar Agung.

Menurut Agung, Pertamina sebagai Soko Guru Energi Nasional merupakan representasi Indonesia yang berpartisipasi aktif menekan emisi penerbangan global melalui pengembangan SAF berbasis limbah. Kehadiran Pertamina pun menegaskan pengakuan global terhadap kapasitas Indonesia sebagai produsen SAF berbasis limbah yang kredibel, sejajar, dan siap bersaing di industri bahan bakar berkelanjutan internasional.

“Di luar pasar domestik, kami menargetkan pasar penerbangan regional dan global, termasuk Eropa dan Asia-Pasifik. Kami siapkan SAF agar siap ekspor dan mampu bersaing, baik dari sisi spesifikasi teknis, keberlanjutan, maupun standar global,” tambah Agung.

Pertamina memastikan seluruh rantai nilai SAF telah tersertifikasi ISCC (International Sustainability and Carbon Certification), mulai dari pengumpulan bahan baku, proses pemurnian (refining), hingga penyimpanan dan distribusi. Sertifikasi ini menjamin ketelusuran penuh, mencegah penghitungan ganda, serta memenuhi standar keberlanjutan dan akuntansi karbon internasional.

“Fokus kami bukan hanya pada produksi SAF, tetapi juga pada pembangunan ekosistem SAF yang kredibel, terukur, dan diakui secara global, serta menghubungkan pengumpulan bahan baku tingkat komunitas di Indonesia dengan pasar penerbangan internasional,” tegas Agung.

Dalam forum tersebut, Agung menegaskan bahwa pengembangan SAF di Pertamina merupakan bentuk integrasi dari strategi transformasi bisnis perusahaan untuk mengoptimalkan aset kilang eksisting sekaligus membangun bisnis rendah karbon. Menurutnya, langkah ini merupakan upaya nyata Pertamina dalam mendukung transisi energi Indonesia sekaligus memperkuat daya saing global.

“Di Pertamina, SAF bukanlah inisiatif yang berdiri sendiri, melainkan implementasi langsung dari strategi dual-growth kami, yaitu memaksimalkan aset kilang warisan sambil membangun bisnis rendah karbon yang dapat dikembangkan,” jelasnya.

Pertamina memproduksi SAF melalui teknologi co-processing menggunakan minyak jelantah (used cooking oil/UCO) di Green Refinery Cilacap, dengan kandungan campuran SAF sekitar 2,4%. Pertamina mencatat journey pengembangan bioavtur ini telah dimulai sejak 2015 dengan penelitian katalis domestik dan telah melewati uji teknis pada pesawat Airbus A320-200 milik maskapai penerbangan Indonesia, Pelita Air Services.

Dalam pengembangan ekosistem SAF, Pertamina membangun rantai pasok terintegrasi mulai dari pengumpulan UCO dari rumah tangga dan sektor komersial, pengolahan di kilang, hingga distribusi ke maskapai. Seluruh rantai nilai tersebut telah tersertifikasi ISCC untuk menjamin keberlanjutan, serta kepatuhan terhadap standar internasional.

“Kami telah menyelesaikan validasi teknis di berbagai jenis pesawat, termasuk pesawat jet komersial dengan maskapai penerbangan Indonesia, Pelita Air Services. Pelita Air telah mencoba menggunakan SAF untuk penerbangan domestik dan penerbangan internasional. Hal ini menunjukkan kesiapan operasional end-to-end yang sebenarnya dalam ekosistem kami sendiri,” paparnya.

Saat ini Pertamina tengah ekspansi skala komersial SAF melalui proyek Biorefinery Cilacap Fase 2, yang ditargetkan memasuki fase on stream pada 2029.

Peningkatan kapasitas ini diarahkan untuk mendukung kebijakan mandatori campuran SAF 1% untuk penerbangan internasional dari Indonesia mulai 2027, sekaligus membuka peluang ekspor ke pasar Asia Pasifik dan Eropa.

Lebih lanjut, Agung menekankan pentingnya kepastian regulasi, harmonisasi standar karbon lintas negara, serta penguatan skema sertifikasi agar SAF Indonesia dapat diakui di pasar global.

“Pasar wajib memberikan kepastian permintaan struktural – yang sangat penting untuk jangka panjang, sementara voluntary market mempercepat adopsi dan inovasi. Keduanya saling melengkapi dalam meningkatkan skala SAF. Kuncinya ada pada regulasi yang jelas, sistem sertifikasi yang kuat, dan harmonisasi standar internasional,” jelasnya.

Pertamina menilai tantangan utama pengembangan SAF global saat ini bukan lagi pada teknologi pengolahan, melainkan ketersediaan bahan baku berkelanjutan. Indonesia memiliki potensi besar dari limbah seperti UCO dan residu POME (Palm Oil Mill Effluent), yang jika dikelola dengan prinsip keberlanjutan dan sertifikasi ketat, dapat menjadi sumber pasokan SAF global tanpa bersaing dengan pangan.

title: Mubadala's Acelen to boost diesel capacity at Brazilian refinery Mataripe by 10%
description:
Mubadala-owned Acelen is set to increase diesel production capacity at its Brazilian refinery Mataripe by 10% in June as part of its 736-MM reais ($143-MM) investment plan this year, Chief Operations Officer Celso Ferreira said.
Mataripe, Brazil's second-largest refinery, accounting for about 14% of national capacity, is set to increase diesel production to 13,700 m3d from 12,400 m3d.
Last year, the country imported about 29% of diesel consumption, according to data from consulting firm StoneX.
After the expansion, the refinery's diesel capacity will have increased by about 25% since it was acquired from state-run oil firm Petrobras in 2021.
Acelen is owned by Abu Dhabi’s Mubadala Investment Company, whose renewable energy company in Brazil, Acelen Renewables, is also progressing with plans to build a neighboring biofuels refinery.
text:
Mubadala-owned Acelen is set to increase diesel production capacity at its Brazilian refinery Mataripe by 10% in June as part of its 736-MM reais ($143-MM) investment plan this year, Chief Operations Officer Celso Ferreira said.

Mataripe, Brazil's second-largest refinery, accounting for about 14% of national capacity, is set to increase diesel production to 13,700 m3d from 12,400 m3d.

The investment comes as Brazil's imports of diesel rise amid growing demand for fuel. Last year, the country imported about 29% of diesel consumption, according to data from consulting firm StoneX.

Acelen plans to invest 4 B reais in the unit by 2030, focusing on maintenance, safety, energy efficiency and decarbonization, Ferreira said in an interview conducted on Tuesday.

After the expansion, the refinery's diesel capacity will have increased by about 25% since it was acquired from state-run oil firm Petrobras in 2021.

Acelen is owned by Abu Dhabi’s Mubadala Investment Company, whose renewable energy company in Brazil, Acelen Renewables, is also progressing with plans to build a neighboring biofuels refinery. Around 90% of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) to be produced there initially is already under contract, the firm said.

The site, with planned capacity for 1 B liters of biofuels annually, is expected to start operations by 2028.

($1 = 5.1502 reais)

title: Drax power plant to stop burning Canadian wood pellets within the next year
description:
Drax power station plans to stop burning controversial Canadian wood pellets and switch to a US-only supply from 2027.
This is on the condition that the biomass pellets are made from waste or low-value wood from sustainable, commercially managed forests.
To feed this biomass plant, Drax owns wood pellet production plants in Canada.
According The Guardian, the company has started reducing the amount of Canadian wood pellets it burns and plans to stop sourcing pellets from British Columbia entirely within the next year.
According to The Guardian, the decision to end its imports of Canadian biomass to the UK was linked to Canada’s decision to impose tariffs on its biomass exports.
text:
Drax power station plans to stop burning controversial Canadian wood pellets and switch to a US-only supply from 2027.

Located near Selby in North Yorkshire, Drax started life as a coal-fired power station when it was opened in 1974, but started co-firing biomass by 2010 in response to government concerns about the UK’s carbon emissions.

Generating approximately 6% of the UK’s electricity, the plant has received billions of pounds in renewable energy subsidies from the government levied on household energy bills as wood-burning is classed as a source of clean energy. This is on the condition that the biomass pellets are made from waste or low-value wood from sustainable, commercially managed forests.

To feed this biomass plant, Drax owns wood pellet production plants in Canada. According The Guardian, the company has started reducing the amount of Canadian wood pellets it burns and plans to stop sourcing pellets from British Columbia entirely within the next year.

The company said its pellet production plants faced a “challenging outlook” after a decision in the second half of last year that, from 2027, the Drax power plant would burn pellets sourced only from the US.

In November 2025, Drax announced it would cease operations at one of its pellet plants based in Williams Lake, British Columbia. At the time it said that “the curtailment and closures of neighbouring sawmills and the loss of a bid for key local supply have led to a significant reduction in fibre availability, thus making continuing operations at the plant no longer commercially viable”.

According to The Guardian, the decision to end its imports of Canadian biomass to the UK was linked to Canada’s decision to impose tariffs on its biomass exports.

Drax’s Canadian sourcing practices have come under intense scrutiny in recent years. While the company claims its wood comes from commercially managed forests in Canada and the US, investigations by the BBC in 2022 and more recently by The Guardian have suggested that some of the wood comes from primary and old-growth forests in British Columbia.

Concerns over its biomass use have now even reached parliament. Earlier in February, MPs and peers from the Labour, Liberal Democrat and Green parties wrote to the energy minister Ed Miliband expressing that they were “deeply concerned” that Drax may have been given “substantial billpayer subsidy” while the company “may have knowingly and consistently concealed information” about the green credentials of its wood sources.
